Title: Mara targets premium salaries for TVET graduates through industry partnerships
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: French multinational company Schneider Electric has signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) to hire Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) graduates with starting salaries of between RM4,000 and RM5,000.
Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the collaboration was part of efforts to ensure TVET graduates remained highly employable while receiving premium salaries that matched their skills and competencies.
"Many Mara programmes involve industry collaborations to ensure our TVET graduates maintain an employability rate of above 99 per cent.
"We also want to ensure these graduates receive premium salaries.
"For example, today, companies are offering an average salary of between RM4,000 and RM5,000 after they complete a one-year apprenticeship with these companies.
He told reporters this after witnessing the signing of a memorandum of understanding between Mara and its strategic partners, as well as the launch of the Mara-SWITCHPRO Apprenticeship Programme at Premiera Hotel here today.
The Mara-Switchpro Apprenticeship programme aims to produce skilled workers in electrical, automation and data centre operations to meet the growing demand from Malaysia's high-technology sectors.
The programme combines theoretical learning, practical training, industry exposure and professional certification from Schneider Electric Industries (M) Sdn Bhd.
Participants will undergo Industry-Based Learning to develop skills and competencies aligned with global industry standards.
To support the initiative, Mara signed MoUs with Bumi Perkasa Engineering Sdn Bhd, Starlite Electrical Industries Sdn Bhd and Advance Industrial Solutions Sdn Bhd.
18 participants from Cohort 1 have completed their training and will undergo industry placements before being absorbed into employment with the partner companies.
Meanwhile, 22 participants from Cohort 2 will begin training in August, with the programme targeting 200 participants over the next three years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Impact Capital partners China's KeenData to advance AI software in Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Impact Capital Holdings Bhd's unit, Impact Business Solutions Sdn Bhd, has signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Beijing Kejie Technology Co Ltd (KeenData) to promote data and artificial intelligence (AI) foundational software in Malaysia.
Under the MoU, both parties will collaborate on sales, business development and customer support initiatives to promote the software across key sectors, including smart cities, smart parks, manufacturing, infrastructure, urban planning, fintech and retail.
They will also jointly organise seminars, webinars, workshops, conferences and trade shows to raise awareness and encourage the adoption of data and AI foundational software among Malaysian enterprises.
Impact Capital non-independent executive director Chyr Ye Hong said the partnership would place significant emphasis on talent development through training programmes, staff exchanges, professional development initiatives and certification programmes.
"Through this strategic collaboration with KeenData, we look forward to combining our local market expertise with KeenData's advanced data and AI foundational software capabilities to support Malaysian enterprises in their digital transformation journey," he said in a statement.
KeenData is recognised for its expertise in AI-native data infrastructure and intelligent analytics solutions.
Moving forward, Impact Capital will continue strengthening its position as an integrated ICT infrastructure, systems integration and digital solutions provider by expanding its portfolio of advanced digital technologies and strategic technology partnerships.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AliExpress hit with US$629 million EU fine over sales of illegal, counterfeit products
Article:
BRUSSELS: Alibaba's AliExpress was hit with a record €550 million (US$629 million) fine from the European Union (EU) on Monday for failing to tackle sales of illegal, unsafe and counterfeit products on its platform.
The fine was the third issued by the EU under the EU's landmark Digital Services Act, which requires very large online platforms to do more to counter illegal and harmful content.
The Commission charged AliExpress in June last year with failing to comply with a DSA requirement to assess and mitigate the risks of dissemination of illegal products.
It set an October 20 deadline for AliExpress to propose remedial measures, and the company could face further penalties if the regulator decides in December that they do not comply with the DSA.
"This is very dangerous for consumers, unfair for companies which are complying with all our rules," EU tech chief Henna Virkkunen told reporters. She pointed to AliExpress's 193 million users in Europe last year versus Shein's 156 million and Temu's 130 million. Temu has also been fined under the DSA and Shein is facing an ongoing investigation.
"One in five Europeans say they shop once a month from Shein, Temu and AliExpress," Virkkunen said.
AliExpress criticised the EU fine, saying it was excessive.
"We disagree with today's decision and the disproportionate fine, which does not adequately reflect our established framework and the significant, proactive enhancements we have made," AliExpress said in an email.
"We are carefully reviewing the decision and considering all available options."
ALIEXPRESS PENALTY HIGHER THAN FINES FOR MUSK'S X AND TEMU
The Commission said that AliExpress had not properly evaluated whether it had enough people to review the risks and had overestimated the effectiveness of its system in detecting and removing illegal products.
The regulator criticised the company's recommender and advertising systems for exacerbating the spread of illegal products and its reliance on one quantitative indicator to measure its moderation system to prevent the risk of illegal products appearing or reappearing in similar forms.
It said the failure of AliExpress to detect illegal products meant that illegal products ranging from counterfeit products to unsafe toys and dangerous cosmetics remained online for many weeks.
The Commission also took issue with the company's ineffective penalty policy, which resulted in penalised businesses continuing to sell illegal products on its platform.
It said that the mandatory AliExpress "brand authorisation" system – intended to prevent counterfeit sales – was ineffective and understaffed and was easily circumvented by traders selling fake products.
The regulator said the novelty of the DSA was a mitigating factor in calculating the fine, which could have been higher. The penalty is significantly higher than the €120 million handed out to Elon Musk's social media platform X in December last year and the €200 million Temu was fined last May, both for DSA violations.
AliExpress dodged a fine, which could be as much as 6% of its global annual turnover, in June last year after agreeing to measures to tackle the dissemination of potentially illegal and pornographic materials on its platform.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EC issues 13,263 postal ballots for Negri Sembilan polls
Article:
SEREMBAN: The Election Commission (EC) has issued 13,263 postal ballot papers for the 16th Negri Sembilan election.
EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said 12,699 of the postal ballots were issued under Category 1A, covering election officials, EC members and officers, police and armed forces personnel, as well as media practitioners.
A total of 343 postal ballots were issued under Category 1B for Malaysian citizens residing overseas, while 251 were issued under Category 1C for agencies and organisations in the safety and health sectors.
The issuance process was witnessed by representatives of candidates from all contesting parties and independent candidates.
The EC reminded postal voters to mark their ballot papers, complete the Declaration of Identity (Form 2) correctly and return them immediately to their respective state constituency returning officers.
The return envelopes must reach the returning officers before 5pm on polling day on Aug 1.
Voters were also reminded to protect the secrecy of their votes by not photographing their postal ballots or sharing them on social media.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit ends higher against US dollar on robust economic data
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended higher against the US dollar and other major currencies on Monday, supported by robust Malaysian economic data that reinforced confidence in the country's economic fundamentals.
At 6pm, the local currency climbed to 4.0895/0950 against the greenback from last Friday's close of 4.0930/0990.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said Malaysia's data points continue to suggest that its economic fundamentals remain robust, with total exports in June jumping 45.4 per cent year-on-year, the third consecutive month of double-digit expansion.
"In light of the strong export performance, the trade surplus balance widened further in the second quarter of 2026 to RM84 billion from RM63.2 billion in the previous quarter.  This could mean the current account surplus balance could go higher in the April-June quarter, which would provide fundamental support to the ringgit," he told Bernama.
Malaysia's trade performance continued to strengthen in June 2026, growing by 44.7 per cent to RM340.9 billion from RM235.6 billion a year earlier on sustained growth in both exports and imports, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM).
Malaysia's exports expanded 45.4 per cent to RM177.9 billion and imports rose 43.9 per cent to RM163.0 billion, with the trade surplus surging by 64.9 per cent to RM14.9 billion in the month reviewed.
Meanwhile, Mohd Afzanizam said forex markets are anxious about the fragility of the peace deal between the United States and Iran, which has led to higher Brent crude prices to US$88.23 per barrel.
"This could mean the lower inflation rate in June could be transitory, which could result in the resumption of higher inflationary pressures in the second half of 2026. This could dent hopes that the Fed may (reduce) the interest rate this year," he added.
The ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies at the close today.
It appreciated vis-a-vis the euro to 4.6743/6806 from 4.6824/6893 at last Friday's close, and strengthened against the Japanese yen to 2.5177/5212 from 2.5208/5246 previously; however, it eased versus the British pound to 5.5049/5123 from 5.5018/5099.
The local note also traded higher against regional currencies.
It went up versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1677/1722 from 3.1709/1758, and edged up against the Indonesian rupiah to 227.8/228.2 from 228.4/228.8, at last Friday's close.
It advanced vis-a-vis the Thai baht to 12.1556/1759 from 12.1743/1965, and climbed against the Philippine peso to 6.63/6.64 from 6.64/6.66 on last Friday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia closes lower on profit taking in banking stocks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's key index closed lower on Monday as profit-taking in banking heavyweights weighed on the benchmark, although sentiment across the broader market remained steady.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) lost 9.16 points, or 0.52 per cent, to close at 1,722.29 from last Friday's close of 1,731.45.
The benchmark index opened 1.20 points higher at 1,732.65 and moved between 1,720.05 and 1,733.76 throughout the day.
On the broader market, advancers beat decliners 590 to 463, while 581 counters were unchanged, 1,101 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover narrowed to 3.27 billion units valued at RM2.21 billion from 3.44 billion units valued at RM3.04 billion on Friday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said regional markets were mixed as South Korean equities declined on concerns over elevated artificial intelligence (AI)-related valuations, while China and Hong Kong stocks outperformed following renewed optimism over the country's AI sector.
"At the same time, Brent crude climbed above US$90 per barrel amid escalating West Asia tensions, raising concerns over inflationary pressures and the outlook for global interest rates.
"While escalating geopolitical tensions and higher crude oil prices may continue to dampen overall market sentiment, sustained foreign fund inflows and resilient buying interest in selected blue-chip stocks should help cushion the downside," he told Bernama.
Hence, he said sector rotation to remain a key theme, with investors favouring energy and plantation counters on firmer commodity prices, while selective opportunities may also emerge in fundamentally strong banking and defensive stocks.
"Looking ahead, we expect the FBM KLCI to remain range-bound between 1,710 and 1,740 this week," he added.
Among heavyweight counters, Malayan Banking Bhd decreased 18 sen to RM11.02, Public Bank Bhd fell six sen to RM5.19, and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd shed seven sen to RM7.75, while Tenaga Nasional Bhd and IHH Healthcare Bhd were flat at RM14.30 and RM8.50, respectively.
As for the active stocks, Inari Amertron Bhd rose by 11 sen to RM2.28, while Pegasus Heights Bhd and Aimax Bhd were flat at half-a-sen and one sen, respectively, Zetrix AI Bhd eased 2.5 sen to 72 sen, and Tanco Holdings Bhd gave up three sen to 30.5 sen.
Of the top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd garnered RM1.46 sen to RM44.66, Vitrox Corp Bhd jumped 41 sen to RM7.69, Westports Holdings Bhd surged 38 sen to RM6.53, Panasonic Manufacturing Malaysia Bhd increased by 34 sen to RM5.64, and Kelington Group Bhd added 28 sen to RM7.78.
For the top losers, Nestle (M) Bhd shrank 78 sen to RM92.66, Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd erased 48 sen to RM18.34, Hong Leong Bank Bhd retreated 20 sen to RM22.24, and RHB Bank Bhd slid 16 sen to RM8.37.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Seven states brace for thunderstorms, heavy rain until 9pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in several areas across seven states until 9pm today.
In a statement, MetMalaysia said the affected areas included Sik and Baling in Kedah, and Hulu Perak and Batang Padang in Perak.
Similar conditions are expected in parts of Terengganu, including Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu and Marang, as well as several districts in Pahang, including Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Jerantut, Maran, Kuantan, Pekan and Rompin.
In Johor, affected areas are Kluang, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Baru.
In Sarawak, the warning covers areas including Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Sarikei, Sibu, Mukah, Kapit, Bintulu, Miri and Limbang.
In Sabah, affected areas include Sipitang and Tambunan in the Interior, Tuaran and Ranau on the West Coast, Lahad Datu in Tawau, Telupid, Kinabatangan and Beluran in Sandakan, and Pitas in Kudat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man caught making multiple petrol runs for Thailand smuggling
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: A 36-year-old man was arrested at a petrol station here yesterday after he was allegedly caught repeatedly buying petrol for smuggling into Thailand.
General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the suspect was detained during Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan/Op Tiris about 10am following intelligence on petrol smuggling activities.
He said a GOF team from the Ninth Battalion had been monitoring the station when they spotted the suspect driving a Pajero.
"The suspect first filled up 89.63 litres of petrol about 9.17am.
"About 40 minutes later, he returned to the same petrol station and filled another 80.835 litres at a different pump," he said.
Radzi said an inspection found the vehicle's fuel tank was still full despite the second purchase.
He said the suspect admitted that petrol bought during the first refuelling was transferred into plastic containers at Jitong illegal jetty in Jeram Perdah before being ferried across the border into Thailand by boat.
"The suspect also confessed to operating independently as a petrol smuggler since early 2023.
"He admitted making four to five refuelling trips daily, depending on demand from buyers in Thailand, and receiving RM10 for each delivery," he said.
Radzi said authorities seized the Pajero, valued at RM30,000, and about 80 litres of petrol worth RM273, bringing the total seizure value to RM30,273.
The case has been referred to the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry for further investigation under Section 21 of the Control of Supplies Act 1961.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC, PNB strengthen strategic ties to bolster integrity in national investment governance
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) and Permodalan Nasional Berhad (PNB) have strengthened their strategic partnership to enhance governance, transparency and integrity in the management of the nation's investments.
MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said integrity-driven governance was fundamental to maintaining investor confidence in funds managed by PNB.
"PNB must continue to uphold integrity and strengthen investors' trust in the management of unit trust investments in the country," he said after receiving a courtesy call from PNB president and group chief executive Datuk Rizal Rickman Ramli at the MACC headquarters today.
Rizal, who assumed the post on July 1, said PNB, established in 1978 as one of the key instruments of the New Economic Policy (NEP), now manages investments on behalf of 13 million Malaysians.
"That means one in every three Malaysians is a PNB investor, and we must ensure investor confidence remains at the highest level.
"This commitment is reflected through PNB's annual participation in the Corruption-Free Pledge (IBR), which forms part of the core pillars of the PNB Group Organisational Anti-Corruption Plan 2025-2028," he said.
During the meeting, both parties exchanged views on PNB's investment procedures, regulatory compliance, governance practices and current issues aimed at strengthening anti-corruption initiatives and institutional integrity.
Also present were MACC deputy chief commissioner (Prevention) Datuk Azmi Kamaruzaman and PNB board member Tan Sri Datuk Zainun Ali.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sunway-led JV wins S$2.13bil Singapore land tender
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Bhd's joint ventures, Gemini Residential Pte Ltd and Gemini Trustee Pte Ltd, have been awarded a parcel of land at Bayshore Drive, Singapore, for S$2.13 billion (approximately RM6.74 billion) by the Urban Redevelopment Authority of Singapore.
Gemini Residential Pte Ltd and Gemini Trustee Pte Ltd are 30 per cent-owned joint ventures of Sunway, a conglomerate with businesses spanning property, construction, hospitality, retail, leisure, education, trading and manufacturing, quarrying, building materials and real estate investment trusts.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the group said the land, which has a 99-year leasehold tenure, has been earmarked for a mixed-use residential and commercial development.
The proposed project is expected to contribute positively to the earnings of the Sunway Group from the financial year ending Dec 31, 2029 onwards.
"The proposed project is subject to usual risks associated with property development and construction, including fluctuations in raw material prices, interest rate movements and gyrations in the property cycle.
"However, these risks can be mitigated by the Sunway Group's and the joint venture partners' experience and track record in property development," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno denies joint BN-PN manifesto for Negri Sembilan polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has denied that Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) are working on a joint manifesto for the Negri Sembilan election.
Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the understanding between the two parties was limited to avoiding seat clashes and did not involve any formal cooperation.
"We have already stated that we are only reaching an understanding on seats that do not overlap, that is all.
"There is no BN-PN coalition collaboration, only an understanding.
"There is no agreement that has been signed. We only have an understanding so that we can maximise our respective victories," he said.
He said this after witnessing the signing of a memorandum of understanding between Mara and its strategic partners, as well as the launch of the Mara-Switchpro apprenticeship programme at Premiera Hotel today.
His remarks came after PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said the newly established PN special working committee would finalise recommendations and input for a
joint manifesto
with BN for the Negri Sembilan election.
Negri Sembilan will go to the polls on Aug 1, with BN contesting 25 of the 36 state seats while PN will contest the remaining 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Velesto cuts short Naga 8 contract after completing 3 out of 12 wells
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Velesto Energy Bhd has terminated its jack-up drilling rig services contract for Naga 8 without cause.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said Velesto Drilling Sdn Bhd has completed Phase 1 of the project, comprising three of the 12 commitment wells over about eight months.
"The termination provides the group with the opportunity to actively market the Naga 8 rig for alternative deployment opportunities.
"The group is currently undertaking efforts to secure new contracts with improved commercial terms, optimised drilling sequence and enhanced overall commercial prospects," it said.
The company said its clients namely PC Ketapang II Ltd, PC North Madura II Ltd and Petronas North Ketapang Sdn Bhd together with Velesto Energy's board agreed to terminate the contract on July 7.
Naga 8 is currently deployed under a separate contract with Jadestone Energy (Malaysia) Pte Ltd for the East Belumut Phase 9 infill drilling project, valued at about US$16.49 million (about RM 64 million) and running from March 2026 to July 2026.
"The termination is not expected to have a material adverse financial impact on earnings and net assets of the group for the financial period ending Dec 31, 2026, after taking into consideration the Jadestone Contract, which keeps Naga 8 deployed, and the group's active pursuit of upcoming prospects," it added.
Earlier last week, Velesto announced that the proposed sale of its Naga 3 jack-up drilling rig to PT Indonesia Drilling Energy was terminated after it failed to complete by the deadline.
The sale and purchase agreement of the contract estimated to be worth US$63 million was also terminated on July 7.
Velesto Energy's shares closed two per cent or half a sen higher at 26 sen, valuing the company at RM2.11 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno maintains commitment to unity government until next election
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has reiterated that it will not make a "tebuk atap" (backdoor) move to topple the unity government before the end of its term, its secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said.
Asyraf said Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi had repeatedly maintained that the party would not resort to such a move, as the unity government was formed based on an agreement among political coalitions.
He added that the agreement would remain in place until the next general election.
"If any party wants to dismantle all these arrangements, then we must all agree to return the mandate to the people, which means dissolving Parliament.
"The context of Johor and Negri Sembilan is different, as elections have already taken place there.
"However, we should not mix up state elections with the understanding and agreement that exists at the federal level," he said.
He was speaking to reporters after witnessing the signing of a memorandum of understanding between Mara and its strategic partners, as well as the launch of the Mara-SWITCHPRO Apprenticeship Programme here today.
Negri Sembilan will go to the polls on Aug 1, with Barisan Nasional contesting 25 of the 36 seats and Perikatan Nasional the remaining 11.
Asyraf said the agreement to establish the unity government, signed on Nov 23, 2022, was based on the need to preserve political stability, ensure prosperity and maintain harmony throughout the current parliamentary term.
He said the agreement was also in line with the then Yang di-Pertuan Agong's decree for political parties to work together in forming a unity government, which initially comprised BN and Pakatan Harapan (PH).
"Remember, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim took his oath of office on Nov 23 with only 112 Members of Parliament, 82 from PH and 30 from BN.
"Only later did GPS, GRS, Warisan and several independent MPs join. No one can deny the role played by any party.
"BN cannot deny PH's role, and PH cannot deny BN's role, nor can we deny the contributions of GPS, GRS and others," he said.
His remarks came after Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday warned ministers, deputy ministers and heads of federal agencies that
they must resign
if they used their positions to attack fellow unity government partners during state election campaigns.
Anwar had said that while parties aligned at the federal level were free to contest against one another in state elections, those holding federal office must maintain discipline and not turn their positions into political platforms.
However, Anwar added that candidates holding positions in the federal administration should limit their campaigning to state issues and refrain from attacking unity government partners using their official positions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Reservoir Link secures gas shipping, LNG import licences
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Reservoir Link Energy Bhd's unit Reservoir Link Sdn Bhd has received the Energy Commission's approval for a gas shipping licence and an import into regasification terminal licence under the Gas Supply Act.
Both licences are valid for 10 years, subject to the applicable licence conditions and annual licence fee requirements.
Reservoir Link executive deputy chairman Thien Chiet Chai said the approvals marked a significant milestone in the group's expansion into Malaysia's regulated natural gas sector.
"These licences, combined with the commission's third-party access regime, provide us with the regulatory platform to explore opportunities within Malaysia's natural gas ecosystem and strengthen our capability to support the nation's energy transition initiatives.
"As natural gas continues to play an important role as a transition fuel, particularly in meeting the rising power demand from the growing data centre sector, we believe these approvals position Reservoir Link to participate in future commercial opportunities while creating sustainable long-term value for our shareholders," he said.
The gas shipping licence allows Reservoir Link to transport natural gas through Malaysia's gas transportation system, while the import into regasification terminal licence enables the company to import LNG into licensed regasification terminals.
Together, the licences provide the regulatory foundation for Reservoir Link to participate across key segments of the domestic natural gas value chain.
Reservoir Link's shares closed down 1.82 per cent or half a sen at 27 sen on Monday, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM101.06 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mi Technovation Q2 profit more than doubles on semiconductor materials
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mi Technovation Bhd's net profit more than doubled to RM55.21 million in the second quarter ended June 30, 2026 from RM15.78 million a year ago lifted by its semiconductor material business.
Revenue rose 46.7 per cent to RM226.02 million from RM154.12 million, driven by a 6.2 per cent increase in the semiconductor equipment segment to RM93.9 million, while sales of semiconductor materials more than doubled to RM131.3 million.
"The performance was primarily driven by robust customer demand," the company said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia.
"This was supported by the continued expansion of consumer electronics in the mobility and wearables segment, sustained momentum in high-performance computing (HPC) and memory, as well as a gradual recovery in the automotive and renewable energy segment."
For the cumulative six months ended June 30, 2026, the company's net profit jumped to RM86.19 million from RM33.6 million a year earlier, while revenue rose to RM393.78 million from RM273.67 million.
The company expects its semiconductor equipment and semiconductor materials business units to maintain their momentum into the second half of the year, driven by sustained demand from the mobility and wearables, as well as HPC and memory segments.
It added that its semiconductor technologies business unit is progressing with chip design through the MiSE Taiwan Research Institute.
"The Vanda 1, 6-inch silicon carbide MOSFET prototype wafer completed fabrication in June and has commenced a series of reliability tests as planned in the second half of the year.
"Commercial production of Vanda 1 is targeted for the first quarter of next year after all reliability tests and certifications are completed, while development of Vanda 2 is progressing as planned," it said.
Mi Technovation's shares rose 3.84 per cent or 18 sen to RM4.87, valuing the company at RM4.38 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mita withdraws tour bus fare announcement after MyCC probe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Inbound Tourism Association (Mita) has withdrawn its ceiling fare announcement for tour buses and vans after acknowledging that the move could raise competition concerns under the Competition Act 2010.
In a statement today, Mita also apologised to the public and instructed its members not to implement, apply or rely on the ceiling fares it previously announced on March 30.
"All tour bus and tour van operators remain free to independently determine and set their own prices in accordance with their respective commercial considerations," it said.
The move comes after the Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC) launched an investigation following a directive from the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry.
Mita said it had voluntarily provided an undertaking to MyCC under Section 43 of the Competition Act 2010, committing to avoid anti-competitive conduct and ensure compliance with the law among its office bearers and members.
"Through the undertaking, Mita has committed to refrain from engaging in any anti-competitive conduct and take the necessary measures to comply with the Competition Act 2010 among its office bearers and members," it said.
The association said it recognised the importance of maintaining a competitive, transparent and fair marketplace for consumers, businesses and the tourism industry.
It also urged businesses and industry associations to ensure decisions involving prices, charges and other trading conditions were made independently and complied with the Competition Act 2010.
Earlier on March 30, Mita
announced ceiling fares for tour buses and vans
, saying the move was needed to help operators manage rising diesel costs after tour vehicles and ferries were excluded from the national diesel subsidy mechanism following the removal of the blanket subsidy in 2024.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: eFishery setback won't affect pension payments, says Tengku Zafrul [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The recent investment loss involving Indonesian aquaculture firm eFishery has not significantly affected the financial position of the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) or its ability to fund civil service pensions, says Senior Political Adviser to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz.
He described the loss as a "drop in the bucket" compared with KWAP's overall performance and fund size.
In a Facebook video today, he said the actual loss amounted to RM163 million, rather than the RM200 million previously reported.
"The entire eFishery investment accounted for less than 1 per cent of KWAP's investment income in 2024 and less than 0.1 per cent of the total fund size in 2025," he said.
Tengku Zafrul said KWAP's total fund size stood at RM195 billion in 2025, supported by RM18 billion in investment income generated in 2024.
Addressing concerns over pension security, he said that KWAP operated differently from the Employees Provident Fund (EPF).
"Civil servants do not have individual savings that would shrink when an investment loses money. Whatever happens with eFishery, the government's obligation to pay civil service pensions as usual remains unchanged," he said.
He assured the public that the government would not ignore the investment failure and would pursue all legal avenues to recover the funds.
"Every cent that can be recovered must be pursued. If there was negligence, those responsible must be held accountable.
"We will not hide failures, but do not mislead the public into thinking KWAP is near bankruptcy or unable to pay pensions," he said.
Prime Minister and Finance Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had previously defended the investment, saying it followed a
rigorous evaluation
process.
Anwar said the investment was based on verified financial statements from a licensed international auditor, while the investor consortium had conducted independent due diligence before committing funds.
KWAP reportedly invested US$47.7 million (about RM200 million at the time) in eFishery in July 2023, describing it as a high-growth technology-based aquaculture firm.
The venture came under scrutiny last year after eFishery co-founder Gibran Huzaifah was sentenced to nine years in prison by a Bandung court after being found guilty of breach of trust and money laundering.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Education, Health ministries urged to tackle rising Type 2 diabetes among teens
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The People's Progressive Party's (PPP) Youth wing has urged the Education and Health ministries to step up collaboration to curb the rising cases of Type 2 diabetes among teenagers.
Its deputy Youth chief Janarthanan Velliathum said the trend, highlighted by the health minister's recent statement that Type 2 diabetes had been detected in
children as young as 14,
underscored a growing public health challenge that demanded urgent action.
He said the disease, once largely associated with adults, was becoming increasingly prevalent among adolescents due to rising obesity rates, unhealthy eating habits, excessive consumption of sugary foods and drinks, and sedentary lifestyles.
"The health of the younger generation is a national asset. We cannot allow Malaysian children to face chronic diseases at such a young age.
"This is not merely a health issue, but one that will affect the country's human capital development and future productivity," he said in a statement today.
Janarthanan called for a more coordinated and sustained approach between the Education and Health ministries to tackle the issue.
He proposed expanding regular health screening programmes in schools to facilitate early detection of risk factors, strengthening health and nutrition education in the curriculum, intensifying awareness campaigns on the dangers of excessive sugar consumption, obesity and non-communicable diseases, ensuring school canteens comply with nutritional guidelines by serving healthier food and beverages, and promoting sports and physical activity through joint initiatives by both ministries.
He said collaboration between the ministries should be reinforced through a whole-of-government approach involving parents, teachers, youth organisations and the wider community to strengthen preventive efforts.
"Prevention is better than cure. Health education should begin in schools and continue at home. When both ministries work hand in hand with parents and the community, we can nurture a healthier, fitter and more competitive generation," he added.
Janarthanan also urged parents to lead by example by adopting healthier lifestyles, providing balanced meals at home, and cutting back on sugary foods and drinks consumed by their families.
He said PPP Youth remained committed to supporting government initiatives aimed at improving public health and was ready to work with both ministries and community organisations on health awareness programmes to ensure Malaysia's younger generation grows up in a healthier environment.
"A healthy generation today is the foundation of a more advanced Malaysia tomorrow. Health is not just an individual responsibility, but a shared responsibility that requires the commitment of all parties," he said.
Ends
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Defence and minerals anchor Pakistan's lobbying push in Washington
Article:
AS Pakistan stepped into the global centre-stage through Iran war mediation, it hired a US lobbying firm to push Washington for boosted security cooperation and minerals investment, the contract shows.
The two-year, US$1.2-million lobbying agreement, filed in a public official database, began in May, spanning a period officials and experts say is critical to converting the goodwill Pakistan has earned with Donald Trump's administration into tangible bilateral benefits.
But Islamabad could face an uphill battle.
"How long the dramatic improvement in relations will last is uncertain given the history of ups and downs" in ties, said Maleeha Lodhi, Pakistan's former ambassador to Washington.
"It is also an open question whether it will outlast Trump."
The contract directs Capitol Hill-headquartered Ervin Graves Strategy Group to engage with the US Congress and government, and facilitate meetings including with Pentagon and National Security Council officials.
Pakistan's latest agreement, disclosed under the US Foreign Agents Registration Act, provides direct insight into Islamabad's strategic priorities.
It outlines efforts to restore defence cooperation mechanisms that existed during the Afghanistan war and attract investment in the minerals sector, currently dominated by China.
"The contract highlights aspects of the relationship that align with Trump administration priorities, including critical minerals and counterterrorism," said Elizabeth Threlkeld, a former US diplomat in Pakistan and head of the Stimson Center's South Asia Program.
The text also demonstrates "an interest in engaging Congress to broaden constituencies of support for the partnership", said Threlkeld.
Powerful Pakistani army chief Asim Munir's rapport with Trump has elevated Islamabad's influence in a bilateral relationship long focused through a security lens.
The US president refers to Munir as his "favourite field marshal", following their acquaintance in the aftermath of conflict with India that Trump helped mediate last year.
A source familiar with the matter said Pakistan's help in US-Iran negotiations to broker their June interim agreement provided a window of opportunity.
"We want to go beyond personal relationships so that the relationship becomes more institutionally based and can outlast changes in government," the source said on condition of anonymity.
Overall, Islamabad's ties with Washington have drifted since the withdrawal of US forces from Afghanistan in 2021 ended a 20-year war that defined much of the relationship.
[[nid:1493429]]
Pakistan instructed Ervin Graves to work on efforts "for the resumption of... appropriate high-level bilateral defence and security dialogue mechanisms."
According to the contract, this could be in the form of a revived Defense Consultative Group (DCG), the forum that underpinned US-Pakistan cooperation during the Afghanistan war era.
The partnership at its height included billions in US financing flowing to the South Asian nation, which Washington relied on for military supply routes and counterterrorism cooperation.
That suffered a blow in 2011 when US forces raided a compound in Pakistan and found Osama bin Laden, the mastermind of the 9/11 attacks that killed nearly 3,000 people.
A Pakistani security source told AFP that defence ties continued.
"While the scope and intensity of cooperation have fluctuated... security collaboration has rarely ceased," the source said, adding intelligence cooperation resulted in last year's apprehension of an Islamic State group operative allegedly involved in a deadly 2021 attack at the Kabul airport.
The lobbying contract dedicates a section to the minerals sector – a global strategic priority for Trump's administration – instructing the firm to "support efforts to identify opportunities for cooperation."
Ervin Graves should "provide recommendations" on Pakistan's policy and investment engagement strategies, and identify possible investors, it says.
That could build on American interest in mining operations in vast southwestern Balochistan province, home to one of the world's largest untapped reserves of copper – an in-demand resource for AI data centre construction.
In December, Washington pledged US$1.25 billion for Balochistan's Reko Diq copper and gold mine.
But Pakistan's least-developed province grapples with a growing separatist insurgency, which has also targeted mining investments by China.
The US-Pakistan relationship would need such headwinds addressed to move past reliance on the possibly fleeting bonhomie of its leaders," said Shuja Nawaz, Atlantic Council fellow and author of "The Battle for Pakistan: The Bitter US Friendship and a Tough Neighbourhood."
"Pakistan needs to get its domestic act together to make itself economically strong and attractive for foreign investments," he said.
"Once that happens, US interest will become stronger."
* The writers are from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asyraf: Focus on policies, not personal attacks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has never used personal attacks as a political strategy in the Johor and Negri Sembilan election campaigns, instead choosing to focus on policies and its track record.
Its secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said neither Umno nor Barisan Nasional (BN) leaders had attacked the unity government at the federal level, adding that the coalition was focused on presenting its achievements and plans for the states.
"The issue of Umno launching personal attacks against anyone does not arise, whether in Johor or now in Negri Sembilan.
"We respect our partners in the unity government. However, we must ask why some parties continue to play up personal attacks.
"During the Johor election, for example, there were claims that if they won, Datuk Seri Najib Razak would be freed. What is the relevance of playing on cheap sentiments like that? Campaigns should be conducted maturely and respectfully," he said.
He said this after witnessing the signing of a memorandum of understanding between Mara and its strategic partners, as well as the launch of the Mara-SWITCHPRO Apprenticeship Programme at Premiera Hotel today.
Asyraf said political parties should instead present their vision and explain what sets their leadership apart in states holding elections.
"We should not campaign by spreading slander, making baseless accusations or repeatedly saying 'sakau sakau' as though we are not part of the unity government at the federal level.
"If any party within the unity government is accused of misappropriating public funds, it would imply that the entire federal government is responsible for allowing such wrongdoing to happen.
"So there is no need to play up such cheap sentiments. Let us focus on offering the people meaningful policies and solutions," he said.
He also dismissed suggestions that Umno ministers or leaders of government-linked companies should resign over campaign issues, saying decisions within the federal administration were made collectively.
"There is no question of forcing any Umno minister to resign or asking anyone from government-linked companies to step down, as these are collective decisions.
"What matters is that the people benefit from that stability and foreign investors continue to have confidence in Malaysia.
"Has Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi questioned the Prime Minister or any minister? Decisions are made by the cabinet, so the issue does not arise," he said.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim warned that ministers, deputy ministers and heads of federal agencies must resign if they use their positions
to attack fellow unity government partners
during state election campaigns.
Anwar said parties aligned at the federal level were free to contest against one another in state elections, but those holding federal office must maintain discipline and not turn their positions into political platforms.
He added that candidates holding positions in the federal administration should limit their campaigning to state issues and refrain from attacking unity government partners using their official positions.
The directive applies to all federal agencies, including the Federal Land Development Authority (Felda), Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) and the Rubber Industry Smallholders Development Authority (Risda).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: A-GC orders inquest into deaths of trio in Durian Tunggal shooting
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Attorney General's Chambers (A-GC) has directed that an inquest be held into the deaths of three men who were killed in a shooting incident in Durian Tunggal, Melaka, in November last year.
In a statement today, the A-GC said the decision was made after it had received and examined the investigation papers submitted by the police and considered all the evidence and findings of the investigation.
The inquest will be conducted under Section 339(1) of the Criminal Procedure Code.
"The purpose of the inquest is to determine the cause and circumstances of the deaths and to examine the facts and circumstances leading to the incident, including whether any criminal elements were involved in the deaths of the three persons," the statement said.
The A-GC said it viewed every incident involving the loss of life with the utmost seriousness.
It said the decision to hold an inquest reflected its commitment to ensuring that all relevant facts and circumstances were examined thoroughly, transparently and independently, in accordance with the law.
It also reaffirmed its commitment to ensuring that all legal actions and proceedings are carried out fairly, professionally and in line with the rule of law.
The three victims — T. Pooneswaran, 24, M. Puspanathan, 21 and M. Logeswaran, 29 — were killed during a police operation in Durian Tunggal on Nov 24 last year. https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/07/1482828/post-mortem-shows-durian-tunggal-victims-were-executed-say-lawyers
Police have maintained that the men were shot after allegedly attempting to attack officers with a parang along the North-South Expressway, forcing police to open fire in self-defence.
Melaka police had then claimed the three were Durian Tunggal gang robbers who were on the way to commit a burglary in the area when they were intercepted by police.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Van service helps disabled, elderly patients get to appointments
Article:
KUANTAN: The challenges faced by families taking disabled or chronically ill members for hospital appointments often go unnoticed, despite becoming increasingly common.
Recognising the financial and emotional strain on caregivers, Semambu assemblyman Chan Chun Kuang introduced Jom Teman Hospital, an initiative providing reliable transport to healthcare facilities to ensure patients do not miss appointments.
He said some caregivers he met during visits to ailing constituents had quit their jobs to accompany family members for essential medical appointments, while others had repeatedly postponed hospital visits.
"Some families had to pay a few hundred ringgit for private ambulance services, which became a burden, especially for patients requiring frequent hospital visits. So, I purchased a van and teamed up with a non-governmental organisation to provide this service," he said.
For patients without anyone to accompany them, Chan said volunteers could be arranged to assist them throughout their hospital visits at RM11 an hour.
He said the Toyota Hiace van, fitted with a wheelchair lift, would pick up patients with mobility challenges from their homes and take them to medical facilities upon receiving bookings.
The companion volunteers include retirees, homemakers and students who want to contribute to the community while earning supplementary income.
Chan said the initiative not only supports patients needing transport and companionship but also creates part-time opportunities for drivers and volunteers.
"The van is not an ambulance and does not provide stretcher facilities. It is a community transport service using a modified van to safely ferry patients to and from hospitals, dialysis centres and medical appointments," he said.
He said the long-term plan was to expand the service by adding more modified vans as demand increases.
Currently, the programme has secured four regular hospital appointment schedules and can serve up to eight patients daily.
Chan said fees collected would go towards the van driver and companions, with a portion allocated to Pertubuhan Komuniti Semambu for vehicle maintenance.
Meanwhile, Khor Kheng Bee, 63, who was accompanying her stroke patient husband Ong Meng Fu, 64, at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital, welcomed the service, saying she paid only RM50 compared with RM300 to RM500 for private ambulance services.
Patients using the van service, along with a driver, are charged a flat rate of RM25 per trip.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Floods, landslides across northern India kill at least 25
Article:
SRINAGAR: At least 25 people died in floods and landslides caused by torrential rain across northern and northeastern India over the weekend, officials said on Monday, with more rain predicted over the next few days.
At least 15 people were killed in separate rain-related incidents in Poonch, Rajouri and Doda districts of Indian-administered Kashmir, while several others were reported missing, a senior police official said.
In the northeastern state of Nagaland, multiple landslides triggered by persistent rains killed nine people, and rescue teams have recovered four bodies, a senior district official told Reuters.
In the neighbouring state of Assam, one person died on Sunday in floods, the state disaster management body said.
More than 57,000 people across seven districts of the state have been affected by torrential rains.
"Active monsoon conditions are likely to persist over northwest, east and northeast India during the next 6–7 days," India's weather office said in a post on social media website X.
In the neighbouring Himalayan nation of Nepal, 27 people have been killed, one remains missing and 69 others have been injured in floods, landslides, heavy rain and lightning strikes, since the monsoon began in mid-June, the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Authority said.
Several highways, including the one linking the national capital, Kathmandu, with southern plains have been blocked due to debris dumped after landslides, police said on Monday.
Nepal's weather department forecast light to moderate rainfall across much of the Himalayan nation on Monday, with heavy rain expected at isolated locations in Koshi, Bagmati, Gandaki and Lumbini provinces.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: World Cup litter at Dataran Merdeka draws criticism online
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The litter left behind at Dataran Merdeka after thousands gathered to watch the Fifa World Cup final has sparked widespread debate online.
The issue gained traction after Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh
expressed disappointment
over the amount of rubbish left at the historic square following the public watch party.
Many social media users described the incident as a reflection of poor civic responsibility, with several saying respect for public spaces should be instilled from a young age.
Facebook user @Lucy Claire JB said the problem stemmed from a lack of environmental awareness and proper upbringing, adding that attitudes towards cleanliness should be nurtured from childhood.
Instagram user @cc010170 described the behaviour as "uncivilised", while @Angelabriones1411 called it "a shameful, disgraceful culture mindset."
Others drew comparisons with Japan, where football fans are well known for cleaning up stadiums after matches.
"Japanese people clean the whole World Cup stadium, but as a Malaysian, I'm ashamed of my own people," Instagram user @kogimeric wrote.
Another user, @mysterious_7106, commented: "If this event was held in Japan, it would be clean before and after. It's all about attitude."
Some commenters, however, argued that responsibility should not rest solely with the crowd.
Facebook user @Celine Dcruz said: "Don't blame the crowd. Make announcements, ask them to clear up and place more bins."
Others questioned the decision to host large public screenings if crowd behaviour was already a known concern.
Facebook user @Kong Ding You suggested DBKL should reconsider organising similar events at Dataran Merdeka, while @Ab Khalid Abdullah Keegan said future screenings should instead be avoided.
Some users also shared personal experiences to encourage greater civic responsibility.
Facebook user @Azliana Azizan said she made it a habit to carry plastic bags to collect rubbish whenever she visited parks or beaches with her children, hoping to set a good example for the younger generation.
"My former boss always made us leave public places cleaner than we found them. It taught us respect, responsibility and civic pride.
"A clean country begins with responsible citizens," Facebook user @Feliscia Ng said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Delivery rider charged with sexually assaulting stepdaughter, threatening cops
Article:
MELAKA: A 32-year-old food delivery rider was charged in two separate courts here today with sexually assaulting his stepdaughter and threatening two policemen and a family member.
The accused pleaded not guilty to all charges before the Sessions Court and Magistrate's Court.
At the Sessions Court, he was charged with sexually assaulting his 13-year-old stepdaughter at an apartment in Bukit Baru on the evening of July 13.
The charge was framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act, which carries a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Rif'ah 'Izzati Abdul Mutalif proposed bail at RM20,000 with one surety, alongside a condition that the accused stay away from the victim and prosecution witnesses.
Judge Rohatul Akmar Abdullah denied bail as no prospective surety was present, and fixed Aug 18 for mention to allow the accused to appoint a lawyer.
At the Magistrate's Court, the accused pleaded not guilty to three counts of criminal intimidation and one count of behaving in an indecent manner by uttering obscene and insulting words.
On the first count, he was charged with criminally intimidating a 16-year-old boy by threatening to burn down his house and dismember his family members at a Bukit Baru apartment on the evening of July 13.
On the second and third charges, he was accused of threatening to dismember Corporal Muhammad Hafiz Zainal Abidin and Corporal Syafiq Salleh upon his release on the night of July 13.
All three charges were framed under Section 506 of the Penal Code for criminal intimidation, which provides for up to seven years' imprisonment, a fine, or both upon conviction.
He was also charged under Section 90 of the Police Act with behaving in an indecent manner by using obscene and insulting words at the Bukit Baru police station at 5.30pm on the same day.
The offence, under Section 94 of the same Act, carries a maximum fine of RM5,000, up to a year in prison, or both upon conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor S. Thiviya appeared for the prosecution before Magistrate Sharda Shienha Mohd Suleiman.
The court set Aug 18 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Danube water levels reach lowest in 30 years, hampering farming and shipping
Article:
BUCHAREST: Water levels of the Danube river in Romania fell to their lowest since 1996 over the weekend, prompting irrigation restrictions for farmers and partially disrupting shipping and tourism along its banks.
Waterways across Europe have been affected by prolonged heatwaves and drought this year and the average peak temperature across Western Europe on Monday was forecast to be 26.2C, 2.7 degrees above the normal high for July 20, the Reuters Climate Monitor shows.
Romania's state water-management agency said the level at the Romanian entry point of Europe's second-longest river was 1,700 cubic metres of water per second on Sunday, against a July average range of 4,700 cubic metres per second.
Reuters footage showed long stretches of exposed sand on the banks near the border with neighbouring Bulgaria, with several ferry services suspended and grain barges standing idle.
Romania is among the European Union's largest grain sellers and an active exporter.
Authorities had restricted farmer access to water for crop irrigation in southeastern Romania and controlled water reservoirs to ensure minimum levels required for cooling state nuclear power producer Nuclearelectrica's two reactors.
Danube levels were projected to increase gradually from Monday thanks to heavy rain in several counties, the water management agency said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirBorneo's state-backed model aims to keep fares affordable, says Abang Johari
Article:
SEPANG: AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd (AirBorneo) business model will differ from that of conventional commercial airlines with the Sarawak government contributing towards fixed costs as part of its strategy to improve connectivity and support the state's economic development.
Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg said the state viewed AirBorneo as an "air bridge" rather than solely as a commercial airline, reflecting Sarawak's geographical separation from Peninsular Malaysia.
"We are operating under a different model. We see AirBorneo as a bridge because we are on an island, and you cannot expect people to swim across the South China Sea."
"We have built more than 20 bridges in Sarawak over the last four years, and AirBorneo will extend that connectivity through an air bridge."
"The model will be different. There will be some government contribution towards fixed costs, while operating costs will depend on the commercial side.
"In other words, the business model is different from other airline models."
He was speaking to reporters at a media briefing following the arrival of AirBorneo flight MY5004 from Kuching International Airport at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1.
Abang Johari said the airline should be viewed as a long-term investment that would support Sarawak's economic ambitions:
"We regard this as an investment to expand and strengthen our economic activities.
"Many other investments are coming into Sarawak, including renewable energy and semiconductors, and there must be a bridge for corporate travellers to fly in and out of Sarawak. That is the investment being made by the Sarawak state government," he said.
Abang Johari also said AirBorneo is preparing to launch flights to Jakarta with operations expected to begin within the next two months.
"Jakarta, very soon. Very soon, within these two months. Actually, we have been allocated the space in the international airport of Jakarta. So we will need two months," he said.
AirBorneo commenced its Kuala Lumpur-Kuching service on July 20 using Boeing B737-800 aircraft operated under a wet-lease arrangement.
One-way fares start from RM375 for economy Ccass and RM736 for business class, inclusive of airport taxes, in-flight meals, seat selection, baggage allowance and fuel surcharges.
The airline is also scheduled to launch its Kuching-Singapore service on July 22, with one-way fares starting from RM399 for economy class and RM699 for business class.
AirBorneo took over MASwings following the completion of the Sarawak government's acquisition of the regional carrier.
The airline continues to operate Rural Air Services in Sabah and Sarawak under a public service obligation while expanding into scheduled commercial jet operations as part of plans to develop a broader regional network.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah seeks RM1.6bil in supplementary supply bill
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah government has tabled a Supplementary Supply Bill seeking an additional RM1.6 billion to finance expenditure until the end of 2026.
State Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said the extra allocation is necessary to cover six main expenditure categories, led by RM856 million for statutory funds.
The remaining allocation comprises RM278 million for operating expenditure, RM210 million for investment expenditure, RM162 million for administrative expenditure, RM93 million for domestic grants, and RM13 million for special allocations.
Masidi said RM700 million of the statutory funds allocation will go to the Development Fund to reduce the deficit in the Development Account, while RM90 million will be channelled into the Statutory Trust Fund to operate and maintain roads and bridges across the state.
"An additional RM66 million is proposed for the State Government Special Trust Fund to finance basic infrastructure projects, including water supply, roads, and utilities, as well as poverty eradication initiatives such as the Rumah Mesra Sabah Maju Jaya and Sentuhan Kasih Rakyat programmes."
Masidi added that RM86 million has been proposed for the Chief Minister's Department and its agencies to fund state scholarships and educational assistance for Sabahan students.
Under the ministry's breakdown, the Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Ministry will receive RM43 million, the Rural Development Ministry RM30.6 million, and the Youth Development, Sports and Creative Economy Ministry RM24 million.
Other ministries and departments will share RM77.4 million for grants to local authorities, the Sabah Skills Development Centre, the Sabah Creative Economy and Innovation Centre, the Sabah State Cultural Board, and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programmes.
For the 2026 Supplementary Development Estimates, an additional RM435,539,780 is requested for 35 expenditure items across nine ministries.
"This includes funding for major water pipeline replacements and concession fee payments under the Sabah State Water Department, alongside the Sewerage Billing System Development Project and sewerage schemes under the Sabah Sewerage Services Department."
The Rural Development Ministry is proposed to receive the second-largest portion at RM62.58 million to finance small bridges and alternative rural water supply projects.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: PSM candidate for Rahang learns lesson from 'sosah' slogan controversy
Article:
SEREMBAN: Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) candidate for Rahang said he never expected his campaign slogan to go viral after the word "sosah" was interpreted as having a different meaning.
S. Tinagaran, 49, said the slogan "Lomah dibelo, sosah dibantu" ("The weak are defended, those in hardship are helped") was chosen after discussions with several parties, including local residents.
"We consulted them about the state's dialect, and we were told that 'sosah' means 'susah' (hardship).
"When the poster uploaded on social media went viral, we were also confused after discovering that the word carries another meaning," he said when met at the PSM Negri Sembilan office today.
Tinagaran, better known as Cikgu Tina, said his team had since replaced the social media poster with one using the standard Malay word "susah".
"Some people informed us that 'sosah' does indeed mean 'susah'. However, to avoid confusion, we decided to replace the poster with the new wording yesterday.
"Only the posters on social media could be changed because the physical posters had already been printed.
"We accepted the feedback from netizens with an open mind, and it has been a valuable lesson for us," he said.
Earlier, Cikgu Tina's campaign poster became a topic of debate on social media due to the use of the word "sosah" in his slogan.
Many netizens claimed that in the Negri Sembilan dialect, "sosah" does not mean "susah" (hardship), but instead means to hit or beat.
Commenting on the issues he hopes to address in Rahang, Tinagaran said several matters require urgent attention.
"These include drainage problems that cause flash floods, traffic congestion and unplanned development around Rahang.
"I find that the work carried out by local authorities in this area has been rather unsatisfactory. Therefore, I hope to beautify the area, including by providing public facilities such as pedestrian walkways," he said.
Originally from Ipoh, Perak, he said this was the second time he was contesting in an election, having previously contested the 2022 General Election for the Rembau parliamentary seat.
"I have lived in Seremban for more than 20 years. To be honest, there were several state seats that PSM wanted to contest.
"However, many members from other branches were unable to commit because of their respective work responsibilities," he said.
He said his previous election experience had helped voters in Rahang become more familiar with him.
"Recently, I held a walkabout programme, and many people told me they already knew who I was, unlike during my previous campaign.
"I take that as positive feedback from voters. As such, I am confident I can compete with candidates from larger parties.
"If I am elected as the representative for Rahang, I will carry the voters' mandate, be their voice in the state legislative assembly and perform the role of checks and balances," he said.
Cikgu Tina faces a four-cornered contest in the Rahang state seat against Pakatan Harapan's Siau Meow Kong, Barisan Nasional's Yap Siok Moy and Bersatu's Tang Jay Son.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AGC orders court inquiry into Durian Tunggal police shooting
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Attorney General's Chambers (AGC) has ordered a formal inquest into the deaths of three men fatally shot during a police operation in Durian Tunggal, Melaka, on November 24, 2025.
The decision follows a comprehensive review of investigation papers submitted by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM).
Under Section 339(1) of the Criminal Procedure Code, the inquest will now determine the exact cause and circumstances of the deaths, specifically examining whether any criminal elements were involved in the shooting.
“The Attorney General's Chambers takes seriously every incident involving the loss of life.
“Accordingly, the decision to hold the inquest reflects the department's commitment to ensuring that all relevant facts and circumstances are examined thoroughly, transparently, and independently in accordance with the requirements of the law,” it said in a statement.
The incident occurred at approximately 4 am on November 24, 2025, within an oil palm plantation. Three men, aged between 24 and 29, were shot dead after one of them allegedly attacked a police corporal with a parang. The officer, who is in his early 30s, suffered serious injuries to his left arm during the encounter.
In May, Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar revealed that police had resubmitted the investigation papers after complying with several additional directives from prosecutors.
The case had previously been referred to the AGC four times for guidance, with investigators recording statements from 63 witnesses and re-interviewing several to clarify critical details.

Title: Premier hails AirBorneo’s RM374.88 fare, warns: raise it and you’ll face trouble
Article:
KUCHING, July 20 — The ticket prices offered by AirBorneo Airways (AirBorneo) serve as a benchmark for other airlines, helping to stabilise airfares in the market, said Premier Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg.
He said when other airlines saw the fares offered by the state-owned aviation company, they would be compelled to lower their own ticket prices in order to remain competitive.
“One of the reasons AirBorneo was established is to address the concerns of Sarawakians, especially during the festive seasons.
“Families often plan to travel, but their budgets are disrupted because airfares fluctuate unpredictably, with prices dropping one moment and surging the next.
“We are at the mercy of these other airlines because they do not have Sarawak at heart,” he said when launching AirBorneo’s inaugural Kuching (KCH)–Kuala Lumpur (KUL) flight at Kuching International Airport today.
Abang Johari acknowledged that while some had remarked that AirBorneo was not driven by profit, its objective had been achieved if its pricing prompted other airlines to adjust their fares.
He also urged the airline to maintain its RM374.88 fare for the Kuching–Kuala Lumpur route throughout the year.
“If you increase it again, you’ll get in trouble,” he quipped to AirBorneo chairman Datuk Amar Mohamad Abu Bakar Marzuki, who is also the State Secretary.
He added that this move would benefit not only Sarawakians but also Malaysians from other states travelling to Sarawak.
Also present were Deputy Premiers Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian and Datuk Amar Awang Tengah Ali Hasan, as well as Minister of Tourism, Creative Industry and Performing Arts Dato Sri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah. — The Borneo Post

Title: Aminuddin urges integrity in Negeri Sembilan polls, condemns vandalism of campaign materials
Article:
PORT DICKSON, July 20 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has called on all political parties to conduct their campaigns with integrity and in full compliance with election laws and regulations throughout the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election.
Aminuddin, who is also the Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Linggi state seat, said acts of vandalism such as burning or damaging campaign materials have no place in a mature and civilised democracy.
“We want to demonstrate the proper way of campaigning because we live in a civilised state. I hope all parties will respect the rules and let the people judge the conduct of politicians throughout the campaign,” he told reporters while campaigning in the Linggi constituency today.
He was commenting on a statement issued earlier today by the PH Media Centre Secretariat for the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, which said the coalition’s main operations centre had received several reports of vandalism involving campaign materials on the second day of the campaign.
According to the statement, the incidents included the burning of party flags in the Palong constituency and damage to flagpoles in the Chembong constituency.
Meanwhile, Negeri Sembilan Police Chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad confirmed that police had received a report on the vandalism of campaign materials in Jempol, while investigations into a separate incident in Chembong are ongoing.
The Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 5, with the Election Commission setting July 28 for early voting and August 1 for polling day.
For the latest updates on the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9. — Bernama

Title: Zahid warns of fraud surge, says Cyber Crime Bill 2026 will fortify Malaysia’s defences
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A total of 8,014 charges related to online fraud crimes have been recorded as of May this year, surpassing the total of 6,140 charges throughout 2025, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
He said the statistics showed that the threat of online fraud crimes was becoming more serious not only in terms of the increasing number of cases, but also the increasing financial losses to the people.
“Therefore, there is an urgent need to enact a comprehensive cybercrime law to deal with cybercrime more effectively, in line with the increasingly complex and sophisticated cyber security threat landscape,” he said when tabling the Cyber Crime Bill 2026 for its second reading in Dewan Negara today.
According to him, a total of 10,245 individuals had been arrested as of May this year, with the highest arrests involving telecommunications, e-commerce, investment and non-existent loan crimes.
He said the number of arrests increased from 16,244 in 2022 to 23,753 in 2025, which is the highest number in the period.
Ahmad Zahid said the arrest statistics showed the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM)’s continued action in combating online fraud crimes.
“This reflects the enforcement focus on the most active syndicates and has a major impact on society,” he said.
The Cyber Crime Bill 2026, which consists of eight parts and 61 clauses, was passed by the Dewan Rakyat on July 1 and was drafted to repeal the Computer Crime Act 1997 and strengthen the country’s legal framework in dealing with the increasingly complex threat of cyber crime. — Bernama

Title: Trailer crash in Sik kills van driver, injures two including former Kedah assemblyman’s wife
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A man was killed while two others were injured in a three-vehicle crash involving a trailer lorry at Batu 34, Jalan Kuala Sin, Sik, Kedah, today.
The victim, who was driving a van, died in the crash at about 8am, while the driver and passenger of a Nissan X-Trail sport utility vehicle (SUV) suffered minor injuries, according to Kosmo! Online.
One of the passengers in the Nissan X-Trail was Datin Norasima Md Nowar, the wife of former Belantik assemblyman Datuk Mohd Tajuddin Abdullah.
Kedah Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division assistant director Azhar Mohamad said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 8.20am.
“The Operations Control Centre received an emergency call through the station’s direct line informing of a road accident involving three vehicles, with victims trapped.
“Upon arrival at the scene, we found a road accident involving a container trailer lorry, a Nissan X-Trail SUV and a Toyota Hiace van,” he said in a statement today.
He said preliminary information from members of the public indicated that the crash occurred after the trailer lorry skidded and crushed the van, before the Nissan X-Trail also lost control and collided with the lorry.
“There were two victims in the Nissan X-Trail, an adult male and an adult female, who suffered minor injuries. Both victims were removed from the vehicle by members of the public before the arrival of firefighters.
“They received initial treatment from the EMRS team before being sent to Sik Hospital for further treatment. The trailer lorry driver was not injured,” he said.
Azhar said the van driver was found trapped inside the vehicle, prompting rescuers to use specialised equipment to remove the victim, with assistance from the Volunteer Fire Brigade of the Yayasan Jariah Malaysia Volunteer Brigade.
“The victim was later pronounced dead at the scene by a Health Ministry medical officer, and the body was handed over to police for further action,” he said.

Title: Sabah viral school assault: Six students charged with rioting, claim trial
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Six female students aged between 12 and 16 were charged in the Court for Children in Kota Kinabalu today with rioting over an alleged assault on a 13-year-old student outside a secondary school that was captured in a video which later went viral.
The
New Straits Times
previously reported that the incident drew public attention after a video showing a group of students allegedly assaulting a Form One student circulated widely on social media on May 27.
The six accused claimed trial before Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus after they were charged with being part of an unlawful assembly with the common intention of causing hurt to the victim using violence.
They were accused of committing the offence at about 8am on May 22 outside the school gate.
The charge was framed under Section 147 of the Penal Code, which provides for a maximum jail term of two years, a fine, or both upon conviction.
The court fixed September 9 for pre-trial case management.
Each accused was granted bail of RM1,000, with RM500 to be deposited, and one local surety who must be either their mother or father.
The six are represented by Datuk Ram Singh and Prabjit Singh Gill, while Deputy Public Prosecutor Arfa Anchal Khan Muhammad Munir Khan appeared for the prosecution.

Title: Oil palm lorry driver killed after being pinned in cab in Jasin crash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A 68-year-old man died after the oil palm-laden lorry he was driving was involved in an accident along the Alor Gajah-Melaka Tengah-Jasin (AMJ) Highway near Kampung Batu Gajah, Sungai Rambai, today.
Berita Harian
reported that the victim was pronounced dead at the scene by Health Ministry (MOH) personnel after he was found trapped in the driver’s cabin of the lorry.
Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Public Relations Officer Mohd Hafidzatullah Rashid said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 11.50am.
He said six firefighters from the Merlimau Fire and Rescue Station were dispatched to the scene with a Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) vehicle and an Emergency Medical Rescue Service (EMRS) unit.
“When the operation team arrived at the scene at 11.58am, they found a road accident involving a three-tonne lorry carrying oil palm fruit.
“The man was found trapped in the driver’s seat and was confirmed dead by MOH personnel at the scene,” he said in a statement today.
Mohd Hafidzatullah said firefighters carried out an operation to remove the victim before handing the body over to MOH ambulance personnel for further action.
He added that the operation ended at 1.19pm after checks found no other hazards at the scene.

Title: Pet project in Parliament: Government weighs fund and tax breaks for animal welfare
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The government is ready to consider the proposal for the provision of a special fund in the federal budget as well as the granting of tax exemption incentives to empower the management of stray animal welfare and lighten the burden on pet owners in the country.
Political secretary at the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) Datuk Azman Abidin said that the lack of a special budget has forced most NGOs and animal shelters to operate voluntarily, using their own funds to manage and rescue stray animals.
“... this matter is actually not just helping the government, but we know that they will make these pets a part of their family, providing them with enough food and so on.
“When there is special funding, we can request them to follow the existing SOP (standard operating procedure) to obtain the funds so that they learn how to manage these animals,” he said to reporters after officiating the Dialogue Session with Agencies, Non-Governmental Organisations (NGOs), and Pemain Industri Haiwan Peliharaan (Kucing dan Anjing) Malaysia held here today.
